response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine geospatial platform experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and has a solid distributed systems background from high-throughput work at Dunnhumby. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and the candidate's C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a thesis project — neither recent nor listed among his skills. His current and recent work is squarely in TypeScript/Node.js, and there is no routing tile pipeline, navigation guidance, or map data transformation experience. The geospatial overlap is real but surfaces only in data collection contexts, not the performance-critical tile generation and latency optimization this role demands.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform development via GISCollective (event-driven geo-data, Kubernetes deployment, GIS web apps for Green Map)
Demonstrated ability to build low-latency, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby using async I/O, caching, and fibers)
Broad distributed systems exposure: microservices, Kubernetes, CI/CD, fault tolerance concepts across multiple production environments
concerns: No current or recent C++ or Rust proficiency — C++ last used in 2014–2015 and is absent from the skills section entirely
Zero routing tile or navigation pipeline experience; geospatial work is limited to data collection and mapping, not routing or tile optimization
Primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js full-stack, requiring a significant language and domain pivot to meet the role's core technical requirements
